L. 111–148, title I, § 1104(a)124 Stat. 146Aug. 21, 1996Mar. 23, 2010“It is the purpose of this subtitle [subtitle F (§§ 261–264) of title II of , enacting this part, amending sections 242k and 1395cc of this title, and enacting provisions set out as a note under ] to improve the Medicare program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], the medicaid program under title XIX of such Act [ et seq.], and the efficiency and effectiveness of the health care system, by encouraging the development of a health information system through the establishment of uniform standards and requirements for the electronic transmission of certain health information and to reduce the clerical burden on patients, health care providers, and health plans.”Pub. children.heading: Code set
children.label: 1 Code set
children.text: The term “code set” means any set of codes used for encoding data elements, such as tables of terms, medical concepts, medical diagnostic codes, or medical procedure codes.

children.heading: Health care clearinghouse
children.label: 2 Health care clearinghouse
children.text: The term “health care clearinghouse” means a public or private entity that processes or facilitates the processing of nonstandard data elements of health information into standard data elements.

children.heading: Health care provider
children.label: 3 Health care provider
children.text: The term “health care provider” includes a provider of services (as defined in ), a provider of medical or other health services (as defined in ), and any other person furnishing health care services or supplies.section 1395x(u) of this titlesection 1395x(s) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is created or received by a health care provider, health plan, public health authority, employer, life insurer, school or university, or health care clearinghouse; and

children.label: B
children.text: relates to the past, present, or future physical or mental health or condition of an individual, the provision of health care to an individual, or the past, present, or future payment for the provision of health care to an individual.

children.label: i
children.text: has 50 or more participants (as defined in ); orsection 1002(7) of title 29

children.label: ii
children.text: is administered by an entity other than the employer who established and maintains the plan.

children.label: B
children.text: A health insurance issuer (as defined in ).section 300gg–91(b) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: A health maintenance organization (as defined in ).section 300gg–91(b) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: Parts A, B, C, or D of the Medicare program under subchapter XVIII.1So in original. Probably should be “Part”.1

children.label: E
children.text: The medicaid program under subchapter XIX.

children.label: F
children.text: A Medicare supplemental policy (as defined in ).section 1395ss(g)(1) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: A long-term care policy, including a nursing home fixed indemnity policy (unless the Secretary determines that such a policy does not provide sufficiently comprehensive coverage of a benefit so that the policy should be treated as a health plan).

children.label: H
children.text: An employee welfare benefit plan or any other arrangement which is established or maintained for the purpose of offering or providing health benefits to the employees of 2 or more employers.

children.label: I
children.text: The health care program for active military personnel under title 10.

children.label: J
children.text: The veterans health care program under chapter 17 of title 38.

children.label: K
children.text: The Civilian Health and Medical Program of the Uniformed Services (CHAMPUS), as defined in .section 1072(4) of title 10

children.label: L
children.text: The Indian health service program under the Indian Health Care Improvement Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: M
children.text: The Federal Employees Health Benefit Plan under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.label: A
children.text: is created or received by a health care provider, health plan, employer, or health care clearinghouse; and

children.label: i
children.text: identifies the individual; or

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to which there is a reasonable basis to believe that the information can be used to identify the individual.

children.heading: Standard
children.label: 7 Standard
children.text: The term “standard”, when used with reference to a data element of health information or a transaction referred to in , means any such data element or transaction that meets each of the standards and implementation specifications adopted or established by the Secretary with respect to the data element or transaction under sections 1320d–1 through 1320d–3 of this title.section 1320d–2(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Standard setting organization
children.label: 8 Standard setting organization
children.text: The term “standard setting organization” means a standard setting organization accredited by the American National Standards Institute, including the National Council for Prescription Drug Programs, that develops standards for information transactions, data elements, or any other standard that is necessary to, or will facilitate, the implementation of this part.

children.heading: Operating rules
children.label: 9 Operating rules
children.text: The term “operating rules” means the necessary business rules and guidelines for the electronic exchange of information that are not defined by a standard or its implementation specifications as adopted for purposes of this part.
